title: Tapping for Rest as Protest
subtitle: An EFT sequence for the guilt and discomfort of stopping when there is still more to do
excerpt: An EFT tapping practice for the discomfort of resting in a culture that frames productivity as identity. Works through the guilt of stopping, the anxiety of not doing, and the belief that rest must be earned before it can be taken.
sourceNotes: Written for homemade.education. EFT framework developed by Gary Craig (mid-1990s).

practice.whenToUse: Use when stopping feels impossible or uncomfortable, when resting produces anxiety rather than relief, or when you cannot sit still without the pull to be doing something.

content.text: A five-minute EFT tapping practice for the discomfort of resting. The script works through the guilt of stopping before the list is finished, the anxiety of unproductive time, and the belief that rest is something to be earned. It moves toward the capacity to stop without needing permission.
New to tapping? Read
how tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop
Tap the karate chop point and say each statement once:
Even though I cannot seem to rest without feeling guilty about what I'm not doing, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though stopping feels like a failure rather than a choice, I accept where I am right now.
Even though the list is never done and so the rest is never earned, I am willing to try stopping anyway.
Tapping round
Tap each point seven times while saying the phrase:
Eyebrow: The discomfort of sitting down.
Side of Eye: The pull of the unfinished list.
Under Eye: The belief that my worth is in my output.
Under Nose: The anxiety of not being productive.
Chin: The exhaustion of never being allowed to stop.
Collarbone: Rest as something to earn rather than something I need.
Under Arm: What happens when I keep going without rest.
Top of Head: The permission to stop that no one else is going to give me.
Reframe to positive
Eyebrow: I can stop. The world will not fall apart.
Side of Eye: Rest is not the absence of work. It is the condition for it.
Under Eye: I give myself permission to stop right now.
Under Nose: My value is not the list I completed today.
Chin: Resting is an act of care toward myself and everyone who depends on me.
Collarbone: The list will still be there. I will be better placed to do it after rest.
Under Arm: I stop. I breathe. This is allowed.
Top of Head: Rest is not protest. It is requirement.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times.
Where this practice comes from
Written for homemade.education. The EFT framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique, in use since the mid-1990s.
